1	# F and S Marine Transport Inc. — Phase 2.8 Mockup
2	
3	Interactive UI mockup for the Batangas ↔ Lubang Island ferry booking system.  
4	**40 screens** at full fidelity. Not live — no data persists.
5	
6	## Stack
7	
8	- React 18 + Vite 5
9	- Tailwind CSS (CDN)
10	- Lucide React icons
11	- Recharts (admin dashboard charts)
12	- Deploys to Vercel

14	## Quick Start
15	
16	```bash
17	npm install
18	npm run dev
19	```
20	
21	Open [http://localhost:5173](http://localhost:5173)
22	
23	## Deploy to Vercel
24	
25	```bash
26	npx vercel
27	```
28	
29	Or connect the repo to Vercel and it auto-deploys on push.

31	## What's Inside
32	
33	| Group | Screens | Count |
34	|-------|---------|-------|
35	| Customer | Landing, Calendar, Sailings, Time Picker, Class Picker, Passengers, Seat Selection, Review + Payment, Confirmation Method, OTP, E-Ticket, Dashboard, Booking Detail, Refund, Reschedule, No-Show Recovery, Emergency Recovery, Travel Credit Wallet, Login, Profile | 20 |
36	| Admin | Ops Dashboard, Bookings List, Schedule, Port Mgmt, Vessel Mgmt, Date Blocking, Emergency Cancellation, Fares, Promos, Manifest Export, Refund Queue, Sales Reports, Daily Sales, User Mgmt, System Settings, Audit Log | 16 |
37	| Staff | Walk-in Booking, Check-in Scanner, Boarding Officer (3 modes) | 3 |
38	| Mobile | PWA Apps Preview | 1 |

**Vercel CLI isn't installed and you aren't logged in. How should we proceed?**

Options:
- **I'll log in via terminal** - You run `! npm i -g vercel && vercel login` in this prompt (the `!` prefix runs it inline). After login I'll link the project and deploy.
- **Use VERCEL_TOKEN** - You set VERCEL_TOKEN env var (from vercel.com/account/tokens). I deploy non-interactively with `vercel --token $VERCEL_TOKEN`. Don't paste the token to me — just set it in your shell.
- **Just build, I'll deploy via dashboard** - I run `npm install && npm run build` to produce `dist/`. You drag-drop `dist/` to vercel.com/new or push the repo to GitHub and import it.
- **Skip CLI — connect GitHub** - You connect this repo to Vercel via dashboard (vercel.com/new → Import Git Repo). Auto-deploys on push. No CLI needed.


**Answer:** I'll log in via terminal

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-23 10:53:04Z)**_

Two issues to flag before I rename:

1. **Vercel project name rules**: lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens only — no uppercase, no underscores. So `FandSBookingApp_mockup` won't be accepted literally. The closest valid forms would be `fandsbookingapp-mockup` or `fands-booking-app-mockup`.
2. **Rename is via API/dashboard**, not a first-class CLI subcommand. Let me confirm what the CLI exposes.

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Vercel project names can't contain uppercase or underscores. Which valid form should I use?**

Options:
- **fandsbookingapp-mockup** - Closest literal rendering — lowercases everything and replaces the underscore with a hyphen.
- **fands-booking-app-mockup** - Hyphenated, more readable. Also affects the default URL slug.
- **fandsbookingapp_mockup-attempt** - Try the literal string anyway. Vercel will almost certainly reject it; useful only if you want to see the error.


**Answer:** fandsbookingapp-mockup
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-23 13:26:40Z)**_

Confirmed — no Git repository is linked to the project (notice there's no "Git Repository" field). So:

- Each `vercel deploy --prod` I've run uploads the contents of your local `vercel_mockup/` directory as it sits on disk at that moment.
- Your git commits and `vercel deploy` are unrelated — pushing to GitHub right now would *not* trigger a Vercel deploy, and a `vercel deploy` will ship even uncommitted local edits.
- Side note from this output: when I deleted/recreated the project, the framework preset reset to "Other". Builds still work via `npm run build` auto-detection, but if you want it explicit, I can set Framework = Vite.

If you'd rather have **automatic deploys on `git push`**, you'd need to:
1. Push this repo to GitHub.
2. Connect it in the Vercel dashboard (Project → Settings → Git → Connect Git Repository) — this step is browser-driven, I can't do it via CLI.
3. After that, every push to `main` becomes a production deploy automatically, no more manual `vercel deploy` calls needed.
